name: Mile-High Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine cocoa powder, baking cocoa, vegetable shortening, and 1/2 cup hot water. Stir in the baking soda.
2. Cream together the vegetable shortening and sugar using an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
3. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in the vanilla extract.
4. Gradually add the cocoa powder mixture to the creamed ingredients, alternating with 1 1/2 cups of sifted flour and buttermilk. Beat well after each addition.
5. Divide the batter evenly among three greased and wax paper-lined 8-inch round cake pans.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
7. Allow cakes to cool in the pans on wire racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans and let cool completely on racks.
8. Assemble the cake by placing one layer on a serving plate. Spread with Cocoa Icing.
9. Top with the second layer, spreading frosting over it, then place the third layer and frost the sides and top of the cake with remaining frosting.
Cocoa Icing:
1. Melt the baking chocolate in the top of a double boiler set over hot water, stirring until smooth.
2. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
3. In a large mixing bowl, sift the confectioners' sugar.
4. Make a well in the center and add the egg white, vanilla extract, and lemon juice.
5. Pour in the melted chocolate mixture and blend until smooth using an electric mixer on medium speed.
6. Add milk to reach a spreading consistency.
